summary: Russia intensified attacks on energy infrastructure, striking Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy and igniting a tanker at Taganrog port. Ukraine reported a drone strike on a residential building in Galați, Romania, and offered air‑defence assistance to Bucharest. Russian forces claimed advances in the north DNR and reported the destruction of an Iskander system and two Tu‑142 aircraft at Taganrog.
key_points: Russia attacked multiple Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy, with attacks lasting nearly a full day and a repeat strike on a Sumy site (Source: @UkraineNow/81534).
A nighttime drone raid set fire to a tanker, fuel tank and administration building at Taganrog port and hit oil bases in Armavir and Feodosia (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Ukrainian forces claimed to have downed 127 UAVs overnight, according to Russian defence ministry statements (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
A Russian drone entered Romanian airspace, crashing into a multi‑storey building in Galați, causing fire and casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81538).
Ukraine pledged to assist Romania with air‑defence support against Russian drones, with Ukrainian specialists to help strengthen sky protection (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).
Russian commander Pushilin reported progress north of Svyatogorsk in the DNR and improved positions south of Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/378166).
Ukrainian SBS forces reported destroying an Iskander missile system and two Tu‑142 bombers at a military airfield near Taganrog (Source: @UkraineNow/81550).

sections.executive_summary: Russia intensified attacks on energy infrastructure, striking Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy and igniting a tanker at Taganrog port. Ukraine reported a drone strike on a residential building in Galați, Romania, and offered air‑defence assistance to Bucharest. Russian forces claimed advances in the north DNR and reported the destruction of an Iskander system and two Tu‑142 aircraft at Taganrog.

points: Ukrainian officials said a Russian drone entered Romanian airspace and collided with a multi‑storey residential building in the border city of Galați, causing a fire and injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81538).
Ukraine announced it will support Romania with air‑defence teams, specialists and equipment to counter Russian drone threats, following the Galați incident (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).
Ukrainian SBS forces reported the destruction of an Iskander missile system and two Tu‑142 strategic bombers at a military airfield near Taganrog during night operations (Source: @UkraineNow/81550).

Ukrainian intelligence noted a reduction in UAV numbers over the airspace, with only a few remaining over Sumy, Poltava and Chernihiv regions, but expected new groups to enter (Source: @eRadarrua/94229).
Ukrainian forces claimed to have shot down 127 UAVs overnight, according to Russian defence ministry data, indicating high Ukrainian air‑defence activity (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
Russian officials reported that 127 Ukrainian UAVs were downed during the night, reflecting intensive air‑defence operations (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
Russian commander Pushilin announced successes in the north of the DNR, bringing forces closer to liberating Svyatogorsk and advancing south of Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/378166).

Russian authorities declared a local emergency regime in Taganrog after the Ukrainian drone attack that damaged a boiler house and left several residential buildings without hot water (Source: @tass_agency/378200).
The Russian Ministry of Defence reported the destruction of Ukrainian UAVs over the Tula region, with six drones shot down at night (Source: @tass_agency/378184).
Russian air‑defence units reported intercepting 13 UAVs over Voronezh region during nighttime operations (Source: @tass_agency/378188).
Ukrainian assault units of the "North" Guards Motor Rifle Division advanced up to 100 m in the Bachevsk area of the Sumy front, capturing two enemy soldiers (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian troops reported advances of up to 500 m in twelve sectors of the Sumy region, with ongoing firefights in Ivolzhansk, Pisarevka and surrounding villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Russian forces claimed liberation of Novovasilyevka on the Kupyansk direction and ongoing battles for Vasilyevka and approaches to Mirny on the Dobropillarsk direction (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian reports indicated continued artillery and aviation deployment around Konstantinovka, with heavy battles in built‑up areas (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian sources noted that the "East" Group was advancing northwest of Alexandrograd village, consolidating a bridgehead north of the river (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Russian statements highlighted progress north of Svyatogorsk in the Donetsk People's Republic, moving closer to liberating the town (Source: @tass_agency/378166).
Ukrainian intelligence reported a reactive UAV approaching Chernihiv from the north‑east, indicating continued aerial activity in the northern sector (Source: @eRadarrua/94250).
A reactive UAV was observed heading toward Chernihiv from Bryansk region, suggesting cross‑border drone incursions (Source: @eRadarrua/94259).
Ukrainian sources noted a UAV heading toward Zhytomyr's Korosten from the north (Source: @kpszsu/63287).
Ukrainian monitoring indicated a UAV group moving from Dnipropetrovsk toward Pavlograd and Poltava, showing operational mobility in the north‑central area (Source: @eRadarrua/94230).

Ukrainian reports described a drone strike that set fire to a tanker, fuel tank and administrative building at the port of Taganrog, with two injuries reported in a nearby village (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Russian forces claimed liberation of the village of Novopodgorne in the Dnipropetrovsk region on the southern Dobropillarsk front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian sources reported a drone attack on a private house in the village of Grekovo‑Timofeevka, injuring two people (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Ukrainian forces noted ongoing attacks on logistics and energy infrastructure in the Zaporizhzhia region, with power outages affecting a large part of the area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/30542).
Russian reports indicated that a UAV group from the Black Sea area was heading toward the Odessa region, suggesting maritime‑origin drone operations (Source: @eRadarrua/94259).
Russian defence ministry announced the downing of 127 Ukrainian UAVs overnight, reflecting intense air‑defence activity (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
Six Ukrainian UAVs were destroyed over the Tula region during nighttime operations (Source: @tass_agency/378184).
Thirteen UAVs were intercepted over Voronezh region by air‑defence units (Source: @tass_agency/378188).
Ukrainian SBS claimed destruction of an Iskander missile system and two Tu‑142 bombers at Taganrog airfield (Source: @UkraineNow/81550).
Ukrainian monitoring indicated fewer than ten UAVs remaining in the airspace, with a few over Sumy, Poltava and Chernihiv, but expecting new groups to enter (Source: @eRadarrua/94229).

key_developments: Russia attacked multiple Naftogaz facilities in Kharkiv and Sumy, with attacks lasting nearly a full day and a repeat strike on a Sumy site (Source: @UkraineNow/81534).
A nighttime drone raid ignited a tanker, fuel tank and administration building at Taganrog port and struck oil bases in Armavir and Feodosia (Source: @UkraineNow/81546).
Ukrainian forces reported downing 127 UAVs overnight, according to Russian defence ministry figures (Source: @tass_agency/378193).
A Russian drone entered Romanian airspace and crashed into a residential building in Galați, causing fire and injuries (Source: @UkraineNow/81538).
Ukraine pledged air‑defence assistance to Romania, deploying specialists to strengthen sky protection against Russian drones (Source: @UkraineNow/81540).
Russian commander Pushilin announced advances north of Svyatogorsk in the DNR and improved positions south of Konstantinovka (Source: @tass_agency/378166).
Ukrainian SBS forces claimed destruction of an Iskander missile system and two Tu‑142 strategic bombers at a military airfield near Taganrog (Source: @UkraineNow/81550).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV strikes on energy infrastructure and cross‑border attacks on Romania demonstrate an escalation in long‑range strike capability, pressuring both Ukrainian and NATO‑adjacent territories. Ukrainian air‑defence successes, including the reported downing of over a hundred drones, indicate robust defensive measures but also suggest high UAV sortie rates. Ground advances reported in the north DNR and near Konstantinovka could threaten Ukrainian defensive lines, while Ukrainian counter‑offensives on the Sumy front show continued contestation of territory.
sections.regional_response: Romania has sought Ukrainian assistance for air‑defence, reflecting NATO concern over spill‑over attacks. NATO officials have publicly condemned the Romanian drone strike, signaling potential collective defensive measures. Russian claims of territorial gains in the DNR may prompt increased diplomatic pressure from Western partners supporting Ukraine's sovereignty.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV flight paths shows increased activity from the north (Bryansk) and maritime approaches (Black Sea), indicating diversified launch vectors. The destruction of high‑value assets such as the Iskander system and Tu‑142 bombers at Taganrog suggests Ukrainian targeting of Russian strategic air assets. Continued Russian advances near Svyatogorsk warrant close observation for potential breakthroughs on the eastern front.
